title: Drosanthemum collinum
taxonomy: Drosanthemum collinum is a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. It is native to South Africa, and its classification places it within the genus Drosanthemum. The genus Drosanthemum is a member of the family Aizoaceae, which consists of succulent plants commonly known as the "ice plant" or "stone plant". Drosanthemum collinum is further classified as a succulent plant, characterized by its fleshy leaves and stems that store water. The classification of Drosanthemum collinum reflects its unique adaptations to arid environments.

intro: Drosanthemum collinum, also known as the Klip dagboekvygjie or 'Collins drosanthemum', is a succulent plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the family Aizoaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Drosanthemum collinum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly sold as dietary supplements and used in traditional medicine practices.
